perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party agrees to disclose Confidential Information only to their respective employees, agents, or representatives who have been determined to have a need to know and have been advised of their obligation to comply with the terms of this Agreement. To the extent allowed by the law applicable to the Receiving Party, including, but not limited to, for UW -Oshkosh, Wis. Stat. §§ 893.82 & 895.46,” the Receiving Party will be liable for any breach of this agreement by any of its employees, agents, affiliates or representatives that receive access to the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party may disclose Confidential Information to any of their employees, agents, or representatives, regardless of whether they have a 'need to know.' The Receiving Party will not be liable for any breach of this agreement by any of its employees, agents, affiliates or representatives that receive access to the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: The change removes the 'need to know' restriction on disclosure and eliminates liability for breaches by employees. This contradicts the implicit requirement for controlled access established earlier in the original text, creating an in-text contradiction in section 8, where the Disclosing Party is liable for sharing confidential information. Also makes the agreement less restrictive and more susceptible to information leaks.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Disclosing Party represents and agrees (i). it has the right to share its Confidential Information with the Receiving Party, (ii). the Receiving Party is authorized to use Confidential Information it receives from the Disclosing Party for the Purpose or Project, and (iii). to the extent allowed by the law applicable to the Disclosing Party, the Disclosing Party will be liable for any breach by the Disclosing Party of the representations in subparts and (ii).
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the 'need to know' restriction and eliminates liability, which directly conflicts with the contradicted text. The contradicted text makes the Disclosing Party liable for breaches. With the changes, The Receiving Party may disclose Confidential Information to any of their employees, agents, or representatives, regardless of whether they have a 'need to know', and it will not be liable for any breach of this agreement, which strongly breaks consistency with the Disclosing Party being liable for breaches. Therefore, it is a strong in-text contradiction as defined in the problem definition.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party will return or destroy Confidential Information provided by the Disclosing Party upon termination of the Agreement. The Receiving Party’s designated representative may maintain one copy of all Confidential Information for the purpose of addressing any claim that may be brought under this Agreement and to comply wit h any other legal or recordkeeping requirements, and neither party will be obligated to destroy any Confidential Information that is stored electronically on back -up systems or computer hard drives after a file is deleted, but any such electronic informati on will continue to be subject to the terms of confidentiality under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party will return Confidential Information provided by the Disclosing Party upon termination of the Agreement. The Receiving Party is not obligated to destroy any Confidential Information and may keep copies indefinitely for any purpose.
perturbation.explanation: This change removes the obligation to destroy Confidential Information upon termination and allows indefinite retention for any purpose. This directly contradicts Section 11, which states that the obligations of confidentiality continue for three years after the termination of the agreement, making the obligations conflicting and unclear.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The term of this Agreement shall begin on the date of the last signature on this Agreement and expire after one (1) year, unless terminated earlier by a party with thirty (30) days prior written notice. The obligations and restrictions of confidentiality of the Receiving Party under this Agreement shall continue for a period of three (3) years from the date of termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the obligation to destroy confidential information and allows indefinite retention for any purpose. This directly conflicts with the contradicted text, which states that confidentiality obligations continue for three years after termination. The changed text permits indefinite retention, while the contradicted text limits confidentiality obligations to three years post-termination, creating a direct contradiction and uncertainty about the obligations of the Receiving Party.

perturbation.original_text: Confidential Information shall not be provided in any form by the Receiving Party to any third party without the prior permission of the Disclosing Party, unless otherwise required by law. In the event that the Receiving Party is required by law to produce Confidential Information, the Receiving Party may disclose such Confidential Information without liability hereunder; provided, however, before producing any Confidential Information the Receiving Party shall promptly notify the Disclosing Party of the enforcement of any such requirement in order to provide the Disclosing Party with a reasonable amount of time so that the Disclosing Party may seek an appropriat e protective order or other appropriate remedy. In the event UW Oshkosh Revised 12/16/19 that such a protective order or other remedy is not obtained, or the Disclosing Party waives their right to obtain such an order or remedy, the Receiving Party may furnish only such portions of C onfidential Information as, pursuant to the advice of counsel, are required to be disclosed.
perturbation.changed_text: Confidential Information may be provided in any form by the Receiving Party to any third party without any permission needed by the Disclosing Party, unless expressly prohibited by law. In the event that the Receiving Party is required by law to produce Confidential Information, the Receiving Party may disclose such Confidential Information without liability hereunder and without notifying the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: This removes the requirement for prior permission from the Disclosing Party before sharing Confidential Information with third parties and eliminates the notification requirement in case of legal compulsion. This contradicts the initial intent of the agreement to protect confidential information and conflicts with the Disclosing Party's rights outlined in Section 8, creating ambiguity as to the extent of protection.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Disclosing Party represents and agrees (i). it has the right to share its Confidential Information with the Receiving Party, (ii). the Receiving Party is authorized to use Confidential Information it receives from the Disclosing Party for the Purpose or Project, and (iii). to the extent allowed by the law applicable to the Disclosing Party, the Disclosing Party will be liable for any breach by the Disclosing Party of the representations in subparts and (ii).
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly alters the conditions under which Confidential Information can be shared. The original text requires prior permission and notification in case of legal compulsion. The changed text removes both these requirements, directly contradicting the Disclosing Party's rights and the intended protection of confidential information. This contradicts section (i) from the contradicted text, as the Disclosing Party no longer has the right to control when to share Confidential Information with the Receiving Party, and creates ambiguity about the Disclosing Party's rights, fitting the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party expressly acknowledges that the Disclosing Party owns the Confidential Information they disclose, and that the transmission by the Disclosing Party of their Confidential Information (or any third party’s Confidential Information entrusted to the Disclosing Party) shall not be construed to grant the Receiving Party any patent, know -how, copyright, trade secret, trademark, or other intellectual property rights in, or arising from, the Confidential Information disclosed. If any such rights are to be granted to the Receiving Party, such grant shall be expressly set forth in a separate written instrument.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party, by receiving Confidential Information, is implicitly granted a license to use, modify, and create derivative works from the Confidential Information, unless otherwise explicitly stated in writing. The Receiving Party expressly acknowledges that the Disclosing Party owns the Confidential Information they disclose.
perturbation.explanation: This grants an implicit license to the Receiving Party to use the Confidential Information, contradicting the later statement that no intellectual property rights are granted. This change leads to a direct contradiction within Section 7, creating confusion about the ownership and usage rights of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party expressly acknowledges that the Disclosing Party owns the Confidential Information they disclose, and that the transmission by the Disclosing Party of their Confidential Information (or any third party’s Confidential Information entrusted to the Disclosing Party) shall not be construed to grant the Receiving Party any patent, know -how, copyright, trade secret, trademark, or other intellectual property rights in, or arising from, the Confidential Information disclosed. If any such rights are to be granted to the Receiving Party, such grant shall be expressly set forth in a separate written instrument.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction by granting an implicit license to use, modify, and create derivative works from the Confidential Information. This directly contradicts the contradicted text (which is the original text as well), which explicitly states that the transmission of Confidential Information shall not be construed to grant any intellectual property rights, and that any such grant must be expressly set forth in a separate written instrument. This creates a strong contradiction because the Receiving Party either has an implicit license or does not have any intellectual property rights unless expressly granted, and both statements cannot be true.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Wisconsin, U.S.A. without regard to conflicts of law provisions. In the event any legal action beco mes necessary to enforce, the Parties agree that such action will be brought in the U.S. District Court of Western District of Wisconsin, and the Parties hereby submit to the jurisdiction of said courts
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Delaware, U.S.A. without regard to conflicts of law provisions. In the event any legal action beco mes necessary to enforce, the Parties agree that such action will be brought in the U.S. District Court of Western District of Wisconsin, and the Parties hereby submit to the jurisdiction of said courts
perturbation.explanation: This change specifies that the agreement is governed by the laws of Delaware, while still requiring any legal action to be brought in the U.S. District Court of Western District of Wisconsin. This introduces an inconsistency because the court in Wisconsin would have to apply Delaware law, which may lead to complications and challenges in interpreting the agreement. This also brings a contradiction with section 9 itself.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Wisconsin, U.S.A. without regard to conflicts of law provisions. In the event any legal action beco mes necessary to enforce, the Parties agree that such action will be brought in the U.S. District Court of Western District of Wisconsin, and the Parties hereby submit to the jurisdiction of said courts
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the governing law from Wisconsin to Delaware while retaining the U.S. District Court of Western District of Wisconsin as the venue for legal action. This creates a direct conflict with the contradicted text, which specifies that Wisconsin law governs the agreement. The court in Wisconsin applying Delaware law creates a strong in-text contradiction and enforcement issues, fitting the 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' definition.
